zh.lifeSummary: 當Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing於1185年出生在比利時時，他的父親Jan II van Cysoing33歲，母親Mabille De Guines - De Marck30歲。他和Maria de Bourghelles至少有5個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1243年享年58歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing于1185年出生在比利时时，他的父亲Jan II van Cysoing33岁，母亲Mabille De Guines - De Marck30岁。他和Maria de Bourghelles至少有5个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1243年享年58岁。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Jean
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Like Jane and Joan , a medieval variant of Old French Je(h)anne. Towards the end of the Middle Ages this form became largely confined to Scotland. In the 20th century it became more widely used in the English-speaking world and enjoyed a period of great popularity, but it is now out of fashion. Among numerous well-known and influential bearers are the British novelists Jean Plaidy ( Eleanor Hibbert , 1910–93 ) and Jean Rhys ( Ella Gwendolen Rees Williams , 1894–1979 ), British actress Jean Simmons ( 1929–2010 ), and American-born actress Jean Seberg ( 1938–79 ). It is also found as a variant spelling of the masculine name Gene .

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: III
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Yuko, Aiko, Hideaki, Hiroki, Hiroyuki, Isamu, Kaoru, Katsumi, Keiichiro, Kenji, Masako, Masayuki.
Japanese: variously written, sometimes with characters used phonetically; the most probable meanings are 仁井 ‘benevolent well’, 新居 ‘new residence’, or 新井 ‘new well’. It is not common in Japan.

en.lifeSummary: When Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing was born in 1185, in Belgium, his father, Jan II van Cysoing, was 33 and his mother, Mabille De Guines - De Marck, was 30. He had at least 5 sons and 4 daughters with Maria de Bourghelles. He died in 1243, at the age of 58.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing est né en 1185, à Belgique, son père, Jan II van Cysoing, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Mabille De Guines - De Marck, avait 30 ans. Il a eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles avec Maria de Bourghelles. Il est décédé en 1243, à l’âge de 58 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing 1185 in Belgien geboren wurde, war sein Vater Jan II van Cysoing 33 und seine Mutter Mabille De Guines - De Marck 30. Er hatte mit Maria de Bourghelles mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb 1243 im Alter von 58 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing è nato il nel 1185 nel Belgio, il suo padre, Jan II van Cysoing, aveva 33 anni e il suo madre, Mabille De Guines - De Marck, aveva 30 anni. Lui avuto almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie con Maria de Bourghelles. Morì nel 1243, all'età di 58 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Jean III van Peteghem Cysoingが1185年にベルギーで生まれた時，父Jan II van Cysoingは33歳，母Mabille De Guines - De Marckは30歳であった。彼はMaria de Bourghellesとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1243年に58歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing(이)가 1185년 벨기에에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Jan II van Cysoing(은)는 33세였고, 그의 어머니 Mabille De Guines - De Marck(은)는 30세였다. 그는 Maria de Bourghelles(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1243년 58세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing nasceu em 1185, em Bélgica, seu pai, Jan II van Cysoing, tinha 33 anos e sua mãe, Mabille De Guines - De Marck, tinha 30 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas com Maria de Bourghelles. Ele faleceu em 1243, com 58 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing родился в 1185 г. в местности Бельгия, его отцу по имени Jan II van Cysoing было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Mabille De Guines - De Marck, 30. У него было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери от Maria de Bourghelles. Он умер в 1243 г. в возрасте 58 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Jean III van Peteghem Cysoing nació en 1185, en Bélgica, su papá, Jan II van Cysoing, tenía 33 años y su mamá, Mabille De Guines - De Marck, tenía 30 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 4 hijas con Maria de Bourghelles. Murió en 1243, a la edad de 58 años.
